'''George Whitby''' (born 17 May 2006) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} for [[St Helens RLFC|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Whitby played his junior rugby league for Blackbrook Royals.

==Club career==
===St Helens===
In round 23 of the [[2024 Super League season]], Whitby made his club debut for St Helens in their 42-6 loss against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] scoring Saints only try. On 6 February 2025, Whitby signed a four-year deal with St Helens keeping him at the club until the end of 2028. In round 11 of the [[2025 Super League season]], Whitby scored a [[hat-trick]] and kicked six goals in St Helens 40-0 victory over [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/24916888.george-whitby-signs-new-four-year-deal-st-helens/|title=George Whitby signs a new four-year deal at St Helens|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/videos/cn84ye41829o|title=Teenager Whitby scores hat-trick for St Helens|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.saintsrlfc.com/teams/first-team/george-whitby/|title=A lifelong Saints fan, who is a promising young half|website=www.saintsrlfc.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/st-helens-starlet-george-whitby-makes-surprise-short-term-move/|title=St Helens starlet George Whitby makes short-term move|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>

===Salford RLFC (loan)===
On 21 May 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on short-term loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=21 May 2026|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=St Helens pair make immediate Championship loan switch|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-pair-make-immediate-championship-loan-switch}}</ref>
